findings.title: Vercel Breached via Compromised Context AI Tool — Customer Environment Variables Exposed
findings.summary: Vercel disclosed a breach after attackers compromised third-party AI tool Context.ai to pivot into a Vercel employee's Google Workspace account, accessing non-encrypted environment variables of a limited subset of customers.

findings.title: Splunk Enterprise RCE via Upload Endpoint — CVE-2026-20163 (CVSS 8.0)
findings.summary: High-privileged Splunk users with edit_cmd capability can execute arbitrary shell commands via the unsanitized unarchive_cmd parameter in the upload/indexing/preview REST endpoint.

noted.summary: NIST stopped scoring non-priority CVEs effective April 15 due to 263% volume increase; only CISA KEV, federal software, and EO 14028 critical software will receive enrichment.
Session and auth tokens exposed in cleartext logs to users with _internal index access (CVSS 7.2); fixed in MCP Server 1.0.3.
Heap-based buffer overflow in redirect URL processing allows arbitrary code execution (CVSS 9.8); niche IoT product with limited enterprise deployment.
New lower-severity vulnerabilities including unrestricted file upload (7.5), MCP config API injection (6.5), and frontend XSS (4.0); less critical than previously reported CVE-2026-33017.
Command injection in WeChat bot interface, missing authentication on logtracks, and hard-coded secrets; niche blogging platform.
Integer overflow in zisofs block pointer allocation on 32-bit systems via crafted ISO9660 images (CVSS 7.5); limited to 32-bit systems.
